name: Goma Joyu-ae (Soy & Sesame Seed Dressing With
recipeInstructions:
-------------------------DRESSING-------------------------
1/2 cup White sesame seeds, warmed and ground into a paste
3 tbsp Sake
2 tbsp Sugar
2 tbsp All purpose soy sauce
Snip off and discard the ends of the green beans and cut them into 1/2-inch lengths. Drop the beans into 2 cups lightly salted boiling water. Reduce the heat to moderate and cook briskly, uncovered, for 8 to 10 minutes, or until the beans are tender but still slightly resistant to the bite. Drain and run under cold water. In the same pan, combine the dashi, sugar, 1/4 tsp salt, a sprinkle of soy sauce, sake. Bring to a boil over moderate heat, add the string beans and return to the boil. Remove from the heat and cool to room temperature.
DRESSING: Over high heat, heat 3 tbsp Sake to lukewarm. Remove pan from the heat and ignite the sake with a kitchen match, shaking the pan gently until the flame dies out. Pour the sake into a small bowl and cool to room temperature. Add the sake, 2 tbsp sugar and 2 tbsp soy sauce to the previously prepared sesame paste and mix thoroughly. Pour the sesame dressing into a large bowl, add the drained string beans and toss together until the vegetables are thoroughly coated. Taste for seasoning, adding more salt if necessary. Serve at room temperature in small bowls, as a first course, salad or part of a Japanese meal.
